message:
feat(ensemble): add findings deduplication engine with clustering

Implement DedupeEngine for finding deduplication with:
- Deterministic similarity calculation (text + evidence pointer weighting)
- Greedy clustering with stable cluster IDs (content-based hashing)
- Canonical finding selection (prefer higher confidence)
- Full provenance tracking through ProvenanceTracker integration
- Configurable thresholds and weights

API includes:
- DedupeFindings() convenience function
- DedupeFindingsWithConfig() for custom configuration
- DedupeFindingsWithProvenance() for provenance tracking
- GetCanonicalFindings() and GetClusterByID() on DedupeResult

Closes bd-34tnr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dmet","title":"Task: Implement output capture for ensemble agents in internal/ensemble/capture.go","description":"# Task: Implement Output Capture for Ensemble Agents

## Location
internal/ensemble/capture.go

## Purpose
Capture pane output from each agent in the ensemble and extract the **YAML output schema** for synthesis. Must handle large outputs and malformed YAML gracefully.

## Implementation
```go
package ensemble

type CapturedOutput struct {
    ModeID        string
    PaneName      string
    RawOutput     string
    Parsed        *ModeOutput
    ParseErrors   []error
    CapturedAt    time.Time
    LineCount     int
    TokenEstimate int
}

type OutputCapture struct {
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    maxLines   int  // Default 1000
    validator  *SchemaValidator
}

func (c *OutputCapture) CaptureAll(session *EnsembleSession) ([]CapturedOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) capturePane(pane string) (string, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) extractYAML(raw string) (string, bool)
```

## YAML Extraction Strategy
- Prefer first fenced ```yaml block
- Fallback: detect `thesis:` root line and capture until EOF
- If multiple YAML blocks, choose the largest valid parse

## Integration with Synthesis
- Parsed ModeOutput is passed to OutputCollector
- RawOutput retained for debugging
- Token estimates via internal/tokens package
- **Do not fail if mode_id missing**; OutputCollector will inject from assignment metadata via Normalize

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Captures output from all ensemble panes
- [ ] Extracts YAML schema blocks reliably
- [ ] Handles missing/malformed output gracefully
- [ ] Token estimation for synthesis planning
- [ ] Configurable max lines to capture
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + parse outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:44.994035314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170149202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170130136Z","close_reason":"Implemented output capture and extraction logic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dmet","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1e5jh","title":"Task: Add tab completion for ensemble commands (modes, presets, status, synthesize)","description":"# Task: Tab Completion for Ensemble Commands

Update shell completions to include the ensemble command family and its subcommands.

## Commands to Include
- ntm ensemble list
- ntm ensemble spawn
- ntm ensemble modes
- ntm ensemble presets
- ntm ensemble status
- ntm ensemble synthesize

 {"id":"bd-1ed7","title":"E2E Tests: Full session recovery workflow","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Recovery

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Clean session save/restore**
   - Spawn session with 3 agents
   - Execute some commands
   - Kill session (simulate crash)
   - Run ntm recover
   - Verify all panes restored with scrollback

2. **Partial recovery**
   - Spawn session
   - Corrupt one pane's state file
   - Recover
   - Verify remaining panes restored
   - Verify error reported for corrupted pane

3. **Recovery after long idle**
   - Spawn session
   - Wait 30+ seconds
   - Kill and recover
   - Verify state still valid

4. **Multi-project recovery**
   - Spawn sessions for 2 projects
   - Kill both
   - Recover each independently

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Spawning session: %d panes", numPanes)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] State captured: %d bytes", stateSize)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Simulating crash...")
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Restoring pane %d/%d: %s", i, total, paneName)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Scrollback match: %v (%d lines)", match, lines)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.783368496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380086093Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380006103Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 5 comprehensive tests: TestFullSessionRecovery (full spawn/crash/recover cycle), TestPartialRecovery (corruption handling), TestRecoveryAfterLongIdle (idle period resilience), TestMultiProjectRecovery (cross-project recovery), TestRecoveryContextBuilding (context validation). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-3law","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33tuj","title":"Task: Add output_lines_since_last activity indicator","description":"Add output_lines_since_last field to pane status.

## What

Add `output_lines_since_last` field showing lines of output since last status check.

## Why

Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing. Useful for:
- Detecting stalled agents (0 lines for extended period)
- Detecting hyperactive agents (excessive output)
- Progress monitoring

## Implementation

Track output line count between status calls:

```go
type OutputTracker struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    lineCount  map[int]int  // pane_index -> total lines seen
}

func (t *OutputTracker) GetDelta(paneIndex int) int {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    
    current := countPaneLines(paneIndex)
    previous := t.lineCount[paneIndex]
    delta := current - previous
    t.lineCount[paneIndex] = current
    return delta
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "output_lines_since_last": 42
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- First call: return total lines (no previous)
- tmux buffer wraps: handle potential negative delta
- Agent cleared screen: reset tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:36:58.734360851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002470134Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002368092Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tuj","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-349x","title":"Task: Add webhook configuration in ntm.yaml","description":"## Overview
Implement configuration parsing for webhooks in ntm.yaml.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/webhooks.go:
   - WebhookConfig struct
   - ParseWebhookConfig(yaml []byte) ([]WebhookConfig, error)
   - ValidateConfig() error

2. Config structure:
   webhooks:
     - name: slack-notifications
       url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
       events:
         - agent.completed
         - agent.error
       formatter: slack
       filter:
         session: myproject*
         agent_type: [claude, codex]
         severity: [warning, error]
       retry:
         max_attempts: 5
         backoff: exponential
       timeout: 30s
       secret: ${NTM_WEBHOOK_SECRET}  # env var substitution

3. Validation rules:
   - URL must be valid HTTP(S)
   - Events must be recognized event types
   - Formatter must be registered
   - Warn on unrecognized options

4. Hot reload:
   - Detect config file changes
   - Reload webhooks without restart
   - Log config changes

## Implementation Notes
- Use mapstructure for YAML parsing
- Support environment variable substitution
- Glob patterns for session filtering

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config parses correctly from ntm.yaml
- Validation catches common errors
- Hot reload works
- Unit tests for config parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.948504334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358287058Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3518e","title":"Unit Tests: Dependency awareness and auto-reassignment logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the dependency-aware assignment and auto-reassignment on blocker completion.

## Test Cases

### Filtering Blocked Beads
- Filter out beads with non-empty blocked_by
- Include only actionable beads
- Handle bead with multiple blockers
- Handle bead with single blocker

### Unblock Detection
- Detect when blocker bead is closed
- Identify all beads unblocked by completion
- Handle chain unblocking (A blocks B blocks C)
- Handle multiple beads unblocked simultaneously

### Auto-Reassignment
- Queue newly unblocked beads
- Assign to first idle agent
- Respect strategy for reassignment
- Handle no idle agents (queue only)

### Edge Cases
- Circular dependencies (should be filtered)
- Bead completes but no new unblocks
- All agents busy when unblock happens

## Logging Requirements
- Log each filter decision
- Log unblock detection events
- Log reassignment decisions

## Test File
tests/dependency_awareness_test.go","notes":"Created /dp/ntm/tests/dependency_awareness_test.go with 22 comprehensive unit tests:

## Section 1: Filtering Blocked Beads (4 tests)
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_NonEmptyBlockedBy
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_IncludeOnlyActionable  
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_MultipleBlockers
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_SingleBlocker

## Section 2: Unblock Detection (4 tests)
- TestUnblockDetection_BlockerClosed
- TestUnblockDetection_IdentifyAllUnblocked
- TestUnblockDetection_ChainUnblocking (A -> B -> C)
- TestUnblockDetection_MultipleSimultaneous

## Section 3: Auto-Reassignment (4 tests)
- TestAutoReassign_QueueNewlyUnblocked
- TestAutoReassign_AssignToFirstIdle
- TestAutoReassign_RespectStrategy (dependency vs priority)
- TestAutoReassign_NoIdleAgents

## Section 4: Edge Cases (5 tests)
- TestEdgeCase_CircularDependencies
- TestEdgeCase_CompletionNoNewUnblocks
- TestEdgeCase_AllAgentsBusy
- TestEdgeCase_StaleGraph
- TestEdgeCase_PartialBlockerResolution

## Section 5: State Transitions (2 tests)
- TestStateTransition_FailedToAssigned
- TestStateTransition_CompletedCannotRetry

## Section 6: JSON Envelope Format (2 tests)
- TestJSONEnvelope_UnblockedBeadFields

 {"id":"bd-352u","title":"Task: Add timeline persistence for post-session analysis","description":"## Overview
Enable saving timeline data to disk for analysis after session ends.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/state/timeline_persist.go:
   - SaveTimeline(sessionID string, events []AgentEvent) error
   - LoadTimeline(sessionID string) ([]AgentEvent, error)
   - ListTimelines() ([]TimelineInfo, error)

2. Storage format:
   - JSONL file per session: ~/.local/share/ntm/timelines/<session>.jsonl
   - Each line is one AgentEvent as JSON
   - Include header with session metadata

3. Automatic persistence:
   - Save on session end
   - Periodic checkpoint every 5 minutes
   - Recover from checkpoint on crash

4. CLI commands:
   - ntm timeline show <session-id> - view historical timeline
   - ntm timeline list - list available timelines
   - ntm timeline delete <session-id> - remove old timelines

5. Retention policy:
   - Keep last 30 sessions by default
   - Configurable via ntm.yaml
   - Automatic cleanup of old timelines

## Implementation Notes
- Use standard JSON encoding for portability
- Compress old timelines with gzip
- Index by session ID for fast lookup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timelines persist across NTM restarts
- Can load and view historical timelines
- Automatic cleanup works
- Unit tests for persistence logic","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyCliff","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:33.281526043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:19:39.965551811Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3lo54","title":"EPIC: Weighted Multi-Project Agent Swarm Orchestration","description":"## Overview

This epic introduces a fully automated multi-project agent swarm orchestration system that intelligently allocates coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) across multiple projects based on their open bead backlogs.

## Problem Statement

When managing multiple projects in /dp, each with varying amounts of work (tracked via beads), manually calculating agent allocation, creating tmux sessions, launching agents, and handling usage limits is tedious and error-prone. We need a single command that:

1. Scans projects for open bead counts
2. Calculates weighted agent allocation (more beads = more agents)
3. Creates tmux sessions with correct pane geometry
4. Launches agents (cc, cod, gmi) in each pane
5. Sends marching orders to all agents
6. Monitors for usage limit hits
7. Auto-respawns with account rotation when limits hit

## Key Features

### 1. Bead-Weighted Allocation
- Scan /dp projects (or specified list) for open bead counts via `br list --status open --json`
- Apply tiered allocation formula:
  - 400+ beads: 4 cc, 4 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - 100-399 beads: 3 cc, 3 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - <100 beads: 1 cc, 1 cod, 1 gmi agents
- Configurable thresholds and ratios

### 2. Session Orchestration
- Create 3 sessions per agent type: {type}_agents_1, {type}_agents_2, {type}_agents_3
- Distribute panes evenly across sessions of same type
- Ensure identical pane geometry across sessions
- Support remote execution via SSH

### 3. Usage Limit Detection
- Monitor pane output for limit patterns:
  - CC: "You've hit your limit"
  - COD: "You've hit your usage limit"
- Configurable pattern matching
- Real-time monitoring via background goroutines

### 4. Auto-Respawn with Account Rotation
- When limit detected: kill agent, rotate to next account, respawn
- Integration with caam (coding_agent_account_manager) for account switching
- Configurable retry limits and backoff

### 5. Marching Orders Injection
- Send standardized initial prompt to all agents
- Staggered sending to avoid rate limits
- Codex double-Enter quirk handling

## Technical Approach

### New CLI Command: `ntm swarm`

```bash
# Basic usage - scan /dp and auto-allocate
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp

# Specify projects explicitly
ntm swarm --projects=flywheel_connectors,ntm,asupersync

# Custom allocation formula
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --tier1-threshold=400 --tier2-threshold=100

# Dry run to preview allocation
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --dry-run

# With account rotation enabled
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --auto-rotate-accounts

# Robot mode for automation
ntm --robot-swarm --scan-dir=/dp
```

### New Internal Packages

1. `internal/swarm/` - Core orchestration logic
2. `internal/beadscanner/` - Project bead counting
3. `internal/limitdetect/` - Usage limit pattern matching
4. `internal/accountrotate/` - Account rotation integration

### Configuration

New config section in `config.toml`:

```toml
[swarm]
default_scan_dir = "/dp"
tier1_threshold = 400  # beads
tier2_threshold = 100
tier1_allocation = { cc = 4, cod = 4, gmi = 2 }
tier2_allocation = { cc = 3, cod = 3, gmi = 2 }
tier3_allocation = { cc = 1, cod = 1, gmi = 1 }
sessions_per_type = 3
stagger_delay_ms = 300
auto_rotate_accounts = false

[swarm.limit_patterns]
cc = ["You've hit your limit", "usage limit"]
cod = ["You've hit your usage limit"]
gmi = ["quota exceeded"]

[swarm.marching_orders]
default = "First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file..."
review = "I want you to sort of randomly explore..."
```

## Success Criteria

1. Single command spawns weighted agent swarm across projects
2. Agents auto-start with marching orders
3. Usage limits detected within 30 seconds
4. Auto-respawn works with account rotation
5. Robot mode outputs JSON for automation
6. Full integration with existing NTM features (health, resilience, agent-mail)

## Dependencies

- Existing: spawn, send, health, resilience, config, tmux packages
- External: br (beads_rust), caam (coding_agent_account_manager)

## Out of Scope (for this epic)

- GUI/TUI for swarm management (future enhancement)
- Cloud-based account rotation (use local caam)
- Cross-machine swarm coordination (single machine focus)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:56:54.050264582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:38:02.407074656Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lo54","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mdhe","title":"Task: Implement mechanical merging algorithms for findings/recommendations","description":"# Task: Mechanical Merging Algorithms

Algorithmic pre-processing before synthesis agent runs.

## Purpose
Because all mode outputs follow OutputSchema, we can do significant mechanical merging before the synthesis agent:
1. Reduce token cost for synthesis agent
2. Surface patterns algorithmically
3. Pre-group related items

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/merge.go
type MechanicalMerger struct {
    Outputs []ModeOutput
}

type MergeResult struct {
    GroupedFindings      []FindingGroup
    GroupedRisks         []RiskGroup
    GroupedRecommendations []RecommendationGroup
    IdentifiedConflicts  []PotentialConflict
    Statistics           MergeStats
}

type FindingGroup struct {
    EvidencePointer string
    Findings        []Finding
    Modes           []string
}

type PotentialConflict struct {
    Topic     string
    ModeA     string
    ModeB     string
    PositionA string
    PositionB string
}

type MergeStats struct {
    TotalFindings    int
    UniqueFindings   int
    DuplicateRate    float64
    ConflictCount    int
}

func NewMechanicalMerger(outputs []ModeOutput) *MechanicalMerger
func (m *MechanicalMerger) Merge() (*MergeResult, error)

// Grouping algorithms
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupFindingsByEvidence() []FindingGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRisksBySeverity() []RiskGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRecommendationsByAction() []RecommendationGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) detectConflicts() []PotentialConflict

// Similarity functions
func jaccardSimilarity(a, b string) float64
func evidenceProximity(a, b string) float64  // Same file = high, nearby lines = medium
```

## Conflict Detection Heuristics
- Contradictory theses (opposite sentiment on same topic)
- Risk severity disagreement (same risk, different severity)
- Recommendation conflicts (do X vs don't do X)

## File: internal/ensemble/merge.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T17:57:40.755425449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:04:27.603528814Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:04:27.602943081Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mdhe","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-3mdhe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

@@ -0,0 +1,516 @@
+package ensemble
+
+import (
+	"crypto/sha256"
+	"encoding/hex"
+	"fmt"
+	"log/slog"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+)
+
+// DedupeConfig controls the deduplication behavior.
+type DedupeConfig struct {
+	// SimilarityThreshold is the minimum similarity for two findings to be
+	// considered duplicates (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.7
+	SimilarityThreshold float64 `json:"similarity_threshold" yaml:"similarity_threshold"`
+
+	// EvidenceWeight controls how much evidence pointer similarity
+	// contributes to overall similarity (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.3
+	EvidenceWeight float64 `json:"evidence_weight" yaml:"evidence_weight"`
+
+	// TextWeight controls how much text similarity contributes
+	// to overall similarity (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.7
+	TextWeight float64 `json:"text_weight" yaml:"text_weight"`
+
+	// PreferHighConfidence keeps the finding with higher confidence
+	// when merging duplicates. Default: true
+	PreferHighConfidence bool `json:"prefer_high_confidence" yaml:"prefer_high_confidence"`
+
+	// PreserveProvenance tracks the full provenance chain through
+	// deduplication. Default: true
+	PreserveProvenance bool `json:"preserve_provenance" yaml:"preserve_provenance"`
+}
+
+// DefaultDedupeConfig returns sensible defaults for deduplication.
+func DefaultDedupeConfig() DedupeConfig {
+	return DedupeConfig{
+		SimilarityThreshold:  0.7,
+		EvidenceWeight:       0.3,
+		TextWeight:           0.7,
+		PreferHighConfidence: true,
+		PreserveProvenance:   true,
+	}
+}
+
+// FindingCluster represents a group of similar findings.
+type FindingCluster struct {
+	// ClusterID is a stable identifier derived from cluster content.
+	ClusterID string `json:"cluster_id" yaml:"cluster_id"`
+
+	// Canonical is the representative finding for this cluster.
+	Canonical Finding `json:"canonical" yaml:"canonical"`
+
+	// Members are all findings in this cluster, including the canonical one.
+	Members []ClusterMember `json:"members" yaml:"members"`
+
+	// MemberCount is the number of findings in this cluster.
+	MemberCount int `json:"member_count" yaml:"member_count"`
+
+	// SourceModes lists all modes that contributed findings to this cluster.
+	SourceModes []string `json:"source_modes" yaml:"source_modes"`
+
+	// AverageConfidence is the mean confidence across all cluster members.
+	AverageConfidence Confidence `json:"average_confidence" yaml:"average_confidence"`
+
+	// MaxConfidence is the highest confidence in the cluster.
+	MaxConfidence Confidence `json:"max_confidence" yaml:"max_confidence"`
+
+	// ProvenanceIDs tracks provenance chain IDs for all cluster members.
+	ProvenanceIDs []string `json:"provenance_ids,omitempty" yaml:"provenance_ids,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// ClusterMember represents a finding within a cluster.
+type ClusterMember struct {
+	Finding      Finding  `json:"finding" yaml:"finding"`
+	SourceMode   string   `json:"source_mode" yaml:"source_mode"`
+	Similarity   float64  `json:"similarity" yaml:"similarity"` // Similarity to canonical
+	ProvenanceID string   `json:"provenance_id,omitempty" yaml:"provenance_id,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// DedupeResult is the output of the deduplication engine.
+type DedupeResult struct {
+	// GeneratedAt is when the deduplication was performed.
+	GeneratedAt time.Time `json:"generated_at" yaml:"generated_at"`
+
+	// Clusters are groups of deduplicated findings.
+	Clusters []FindingCluster `json:"clusters" yaml:"clusters"`
+
+	// Stats provides deduplication statistics.
+	Stats DedupeStats `json:"stats" yaml:"stats"`
+
+	// Config records the configuration used.
+	Config DedupeConfig `json:"config" yaml:"config"`
+}
+
+// DedupeStats provides statistics about the deduplication operation.
+type DedupeStats struct {
+	// InputFindings is the total number of findings before deduplication.
+	InputFindings int `json:"input_findings" yaml:"input_findings"`
+
+	// OutputClusters is the number of clusters produced.
+	OutputClusters int `json:"output_clusters" yaml:"output_clusters"`
+
+	// DuplicatesFound is the number of duplicates detected.
+	DuplicatesFound int `json:"duplicates_found" yaml:"duplicates_found"`
+
+	// ReductionPercent is the percentage reduction (0-100).
+	ReductionPercent float64 `json:"reduction_percent" yaml:"reduction_percent"`
+
+	// LargestCluster is the size of the biggest cluster.
+	LargestCluster int `json:"largest_cluster" yaml:"largest_cluster"`
+
+	// AverageSimilarity is the mean similarity within clusters.
+	AverageSimilarity float64 `json:"average_similarity" yaml:"average_similarity"`
+
+	// ProcessingTime is how long deduplication took.
+	ProcessingTime time.Duration `json:"processing_time" yaml:"processing_time"`
+}
+
+// DedupeEngine performs finding deduplication with clustering.
+type DedupeEngine struct {
+	config  DedupeConfig
+	tracker *ProvenanceTracker
+}
+
+// dedupeFindingEntry is an internal type for tracking findings during deduplication.
+type dedupeFindingEntry struct {
+	finding      Finding
+	sourceMode   string
+	provenanceID string
+}
+
+// NewDedupeEngine creates a deduplication engine with the given config.
+func NewDedupeEngine(cfg DedupeConfig) *DedupeEngine {
+	return &DedupeEngine{
+		config: cfg,
+	}
+}
+
+// NewDedupeEngineWithProvenance creates a deduplication engine that tracks provenance.
+func NewDedupeEngineWithProvenance(cfg DedupeConfig, tracker *ProvenanceTracker) *DedupeEngine {
+	return &DedupeEngine{
+		config:  cfg,
+		tracker: tracker,
+	}
+}
+
+// Dedupe performs deduplication on findings from multiple mode outputs.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) Dedupe(outputs []ModeOutput) *DedupeResult {
+	start := time.Now()
+
+	slog.Info("dedupe engine starting",
+		"input_modes", len(outputs),
+		"threshold", e.config.SimilarityThreshold,
+	)
+
+	// Collect all findings with metadata
+	var allFindings []dedupeFindingEntry
+
+	for _, output := range outputs {
+		for _, f := range output.TopFindings {
+			provID := ""
+			if e.tracker != nil && e.config.PreserveProvenance {
+				provID = e.tracker.RecordDiscovery(output.ModeID, f)
+			}
+			allFindings = append(allFindings, dedupeFindingEntry{
+				finding:      f,
+				sourceMode:   output.ModeID,
+				provenanceID: provID,
+			})
+		}
+	}
+
+	if len(allFindings) == 0 {
+		return &DedupeResult{
+			GeneratedAt: time.Now(),
+			Clusters:    nil,
+			Stats: DedupeStats{
+				InputFindings:  0,
+				OutputClusters: 0,
+				ProcessingTime: time.Since(start),
+			},
+			Config: e.config,
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Build clusters using greedy clustering
+	clusters := e.buildClusters(allFindings)
+
+	// Compute statistics
+	inputCount := len(allFindings)
+	outputCount := len(clusters)
+	duplicates := inputCount - outputCount
+
+	reductionPct := 0.0
+	if inputCount > 0 {
+		reductionPct = float64(duplicates) / float64(inputCount) * 100
+	}
+
+	largestCluster := 0
+	var totalSim float64
+	var simCount int
+
+	for _, c := range clusters {
+		if c.MemberCount > largestCluster {
+			largestCluster = c.MemberCount
+		}
+		for _, m := range c.Members {
+			if m.Similarity > 0 && m.Similarity < 1.0 {
+				totalSim += m.Similarity
+				simCount++
+			}
+		}
+	}
+
+	avgSim := 0.0
+	if simCount > 0 {
+		avgSim = totalSim / float64(simCount)
+	}
+
+	slog.Info("dedupe engine completed",
+		"input_findings", inputCount,
+		"output_clusters", outputCount,
+		"duplicates", duplicates,
+		"reduction_percent", fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%%", reductionPct),
+		"duration", time.Since(start),
+	)
+
+	return &DedupeResult{
+		GeneratedAt: time.Now(),
+		Clusters:    clusters,
+		Stats: DedupeStats{
+			InputFindings:     inputCount,
+			OutputClusters:    outputCount,
+			DuplicatesFound:   duplicates,
+			ReductionPercent:  reductionPct,
+			LargestCluster:    largestCluster,
+			AverageSimilarity: avgSim,
+			ProcessingTime:    time.Since(start),
+		},
+		Config: e.config,
+	}
+}
+
+// buildClusters groups similar findings into clusters.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) buildClusters(findings []dedupeFindingEntry) []FindingCluster {
+	if len(findings) == 0 {
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	// Track which findings have been assigned to a cluster
+	assigned := make([]bool, len(findings))
+	var clusters []FindingCluster
+
+	for i := 0; i < len(findings); i++ {
+		if assigned[i] {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		// Start a new cluster with this finding
+		cluster := e.startCluster(findings[i])
+		assigned[i] = true
+
+		// Find similar findings to add to this cluster
+		for j := i + 1; j < len(findings); j++ {
+			if assigned[j] {
+				continue
+			}
+
+			sim := e.computeSimilarity(findings[i].finding, findings[j].finding)
+			if sim >= e.config.SimilarityThreshold {
+				e.addToCluster(&cluster, findings[j], sim)
+				assigned[j] = true
+
+				// Record merge in provenance
+				if e.tracker != nil && e.config.PreserveProvenance {
+					if findings[i].provenanceID != "" && findings[j].provenanceID != "" {
+						_ = e.tracker.RecordMerge(
+							findings[i].provenanceID,
+							[]string{findings[j].provenanceID},
+							sim,
+						)
+					}
+				}
+			}
+		}
+
+		// Finalize cluster
+		e.finalizeCluster(&cluster)
+		clusters = append(clusters, cluster)
+	}
+
+	// Sort clusters deterministically by cluster ID
+	sort.Slice(clusters, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return clusters[i].ClusterID < clusters[j].ClusterID
+	})
+
+	return clusters
+}
+
+// startCluster creates a new cluster with an initial finding.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) startCluster(entry dedupeFindingEntry) FindingCluster {
+	member := ClusterMember{
+		Finding:      entry.finding,
+		SourceMode:   entry.sourceMode,
+		Similarity:   1.0, // Self-similarity
+		ProvenanceID: entry.provenanceID,
+	}
+
+	return FindingCluster{
+		Canonical:   entry.finding,
+		Members:     []ClusterMember{member},
+		SourceModes: []string{entry.sourceMode},
+	}
+}
+
+// addToCluster adds a finding to an existing cluster.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) addToCluster(cluster *FindingCluster, entry dedupeFindingEntry, similarity float64) {
+	member := ClusterMember{
+		Finding:      entry.finding,
+		SourceMode:   entry.sourceMode,
+		Similarity:   similarity,
+		ProvenanceID: entry.provenanceID,
+	}
+	cluster.Members = append(cluster.Members, member)
+
+	// Add source mode if not already present
+	found := false
+	for _, m := range cluster.SourceModes {
+		if m == entry.sourceMode {
+			found = true
+			break
+		}
+	}
+	if !found {
+		cluster.SourceModes = append(cluster.SourceModes, entry.sourceMode)
+	}
+
+	// Update canonical if this has higher confidence
+	if e.config.PreferHighConfidence && entry.finding.Confidence > cluster.Canonical.Confidence {
+		cluster.Canonical = entry.finding
+	}
+}
+
+// finalizeCluster computes derived fields and generates stable cluster ID.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) finalizeCluster(cluster *FindingCluster) {
+	// Sort members deterministically (by source mode, then finding text)
+	sort.Slice(cluster.Members, func(i, j int) bool {
+		if cluster.Members[i].SourceMode != cluster.Members[j].SourceMode {
+			return cluster.Members[i].SourceMode < cluster.Members[j].SourceMode
+		}
+		return cluster.Members[i].Finding.Finding < cluster.Members[j].Finding.Finding
+	})
+
+	// Sort source modes
+	sort.Strings(cluster.SourceModes)
+
+	// Compute member count
+	cluster.MemberCount = len(cluster.Members)
+
+	// Compute confidence stats
+	var sumConf, maxConf Confidence
+	for _, m := range cluster.Members {
+		sumConf += m.Finding.Confidence
+		if m.Finding.Confidence > maxConf {
+			maxConf = m.Finding.Confidence
+		}
+	}
+	if cluster.MemberCount > 0 {
+		cluster.AverageConfidence = sumConf / Confidence(cluster.MemberCount)
+	}
+	cluster.MaxConfidence = maxConf
+
+	// Collect provenance IDs
+	for _, m := range cluster.Members {
+		if m.ProvenanceID != "" {
+			cluster.ProvenanceIDs = append(cluster.ProvenanceIDs, m.ProvenanceID)
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Generate stable cluster ID from content hash
+	cluster.ClusterID = e.generateClusterID(cluster)
+}
+
+// generateClusterID creates a stable hash-based ID for the cluster.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) generateClusterID(cluster *FindingCluster) string {
+	h := sha256.New()
+
+	// Include all member finding texts in sorted order for stability
+	texts := make([]string, len(cluster.Members))
+	for i, m := range cluster.Members {
+		texts[i] = m.Finding.Finding
+	}
+	sort.Strings(texts)
+
+	for _, t := range texts {
+		h.Write([]byte(t))
+		h.Write([]byte{0})
+	}
+
+	return "clu-" + hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))[:8]
+}
+
+// computeSimilarity calculates similarity between two findings.
+func (e *DedupeEngine) computeSimilarity(a, b Finding) float64 {
+	// Normalize weights
+	totalWeight := e.config.TextWeight + e.config.EvidenceWeight
+	if totalWeight == 0 {
+		totalWeight = 1.0
+	}
+	textWeight := e.config.TextWeight / totalWeight
+	evidenceWeight := e.config.EvidenceWeight / totalWeight
+
+	// Text similarity using Jaccard
+	textA := normalizeText(a.Finding)
+	textB := normalizeText(b.Finding)
+	tokensA := tokenize(textA)
+	tokensB := tokenize(textB)
+	textSim := jaccardSimilarity(tokensA, tokensB)
+
+	// Evidence similarity
+	evidenceSim := evidenceProximity(a.EvidencePointer, b.EvidencePointer)
+
+	// Combined weighted similarity
+	combined := textWeight*textSim + evidenceWeight*evidenceSim
+
+	slog.Debug("dedupe similarity computed",
+		"text_sim", fmt.Sprintf("%.3f", textSim),
+		"evidence_sim", fmt.Sprintf("%.3f", evidenceSim),
+		"combined", fmt.Sprintf("%.3f", combined),
+	)
+
+	return combined
+}
+
+// GetCanonicalFindings returns just the canonical finding from each cluster.
+func (r *DedupeResult) GetCanonicalFindings() []Finding {
+	if r == nil {
+		return nil
+	}
+	findings := make([]Finding, len(r.Clusters))
+	for i, c := range r.Clusters {
+		findings[i] = c.Canonical
+	}
+	return findings
+}
+
+// GetClusterByID returns a cluster by its ID, or nil if not found.
+func (r *DedupeResult) GetClusterByID(id string) *FindingCluster {
+	if r == nil {
+		return nil
+	}
+	for i := range r.Clusters {
+		if r.Clusters[i].ClusterID == id {
+			return &r.Clusters[i]
+		}
+	}
+	return nil
+}
+
+// Render produces a human-readable summary of deduplication results.
+func (r *DedupeResult) Render() string {
+	if r == nil {
+		return "No deduplication results"
+	}
+
+	var b strings.Builder
+	b.WriteString("Deduplication Results:\n")
+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("-", 50) + "\n\n")
+
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Input Findings:    %d\n", r.Stats.InputFindings)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Output Clusters:   %d\n", r.Stats.OutputClusters)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Duplicates Found:  %d\n", r.Stats.DuplicatesFound)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Reduction:         %.1f%%\n", r.Stats.ReductionPercent)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Largest Cluster:   %d members\n", r.Stats.LargestCluster)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Avg Similarity:    %.2f\n", r.Stats.AverageSimilarity)
+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Processing Time:   %s\n\n", r.Stats.ProcessingTime)
+
+	b.WriteString("Clusters:\n")
+	for i, c := range r.Clusters {
+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\n%d. [%s] (%d members, conf: %.2f)\n",
+			i+1, c.ClusterID, c.MemberCount, c.MaxConfidence)
+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "   Canonical: %s\n", truncateDedupText(c.Canonical.Finding, 60))
+		if c.Canonical.EvidencePointer != "" {
+			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "   Evidence: %s\n", c.Canonical.EvidencePointer)
+		}
+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "   Sources: %s\n", strings.Join(c.SourceModes, ", "))
+	}
+
+	return b.String()
+}
+
+// truncateDedupText limits text length with ellipsis.
+// Named differently to avoid conflict with provenance.go.
+func truncateDedupText(s string, maxLen int) string {
+	if len(s) <= maxLen {
+		return s
+	}
+	return s[:maxLen-3] + "..."
+}
+
+// DedupeFindings is a convenience function for deduplicating findings.
+func DedupeFindings(outputs []ModeOutput) *DedupeResult {
+	return NewDedupeEngine(DefaultDedupeConfig()).Dedupe(outputs)
+}
+
+// DedupeFindingsWithConfig allows custom configuration.
+func DedupeFindingsWithConfig(outputs []ModeOutput, cfg DedupeConfig) *DedupeResult {
+	return NewDedupeEngine(cfg).Dedupe(outputs)
+}
+
+// DedupeFindingsWithProvenance includes provenance tracking.
+func DedupeFindingsWithProvenance(outputs []ModeOutput, cfg DedupeConfig, tracker *ProvenanceTracker) *DedupeResult {
+	return NewDedupeEngineWithProvenance(cfg, tracker).Dedupe(outputs)
+}
